title: Elio and the Mystery of the Missing Lunch
synopsis: When Elio's lunch mysteriously disappears, he teams up with his diverse group of friends to solve the case. Their investigation leads to silly suspects and surprising discoveries, teaching them about friendship and sharing along the way.

pages.text: Elio sat at his favorite lunch table. He opened his blue lunchbox. "Oh no!" he said. "My sandwich is gone!" Elio had curly brown hair and big brown eyes. Today his eyes were very wide with surprise.

illustration.description: A young Latino boy with curly brown hair and big expressive brown eyes sits at a colorful school lunch table, staring into an empty blue lunchbox with shock. The cafeteria background shows other diverse children eating lunch at bright tables.
illustration.alt: Elio looking shocked at his empty lunchbox

pages.text: "What happened to your lunch?" asked Maya. She had long black hair and kind eyes. Next to her sat Jin with his red glasses. "This is a mystery!" said Jin. "We need to find clues."

illustration.description: Maya, a girl with long straight black hair and gentle dark eyes, sits next to Jin, an Asian boy with bright red glasses and spiky black hair. They both look concerned and curious, leaning toward Elio at the lunch table.
illustration.alt: Maya and Jin talking to Elio about the missing lunch

pages.text: Elio felt sad. His mom made the best peanut butter and jelly sandwiches. "Who would take my lunch?" he wondered. Zara came over with her lunch tray. She had beautiful dark skin and wore colorful hair clips. "We will help you find it!" she said with a big smile.

illustration.description: Zara, a cheerful Black girl with dark skin, bright smile, and colorful butterfly hair clips in her natural hair, approaches the group carrying a lunch tray. Her expression is determined and friendly.
illustration.alt: Zara offering to help find Elio's missing lunch

pages.text: The four friends became detectives. They looked under the table. They looked in the trash can. "Yuck!" said Maya. "No sandwich here. Just banana peels and empty milk boxes."

illustration.description: The four diverse children crawl around looking under lunch tables and peering into trash cans. Maya holds her nose while looking disgusted. The scene is colorful and playful with exaggerated expressions of determination and mild disgust.
illustration.alt: Friends searching for clues around the cafeteria

pages.text: Jin found breadcrumbs on the floor. "Look!" he said, pointing with his finger. "The crumbs make a trail!" The trail led across the cafeteria. It went past the milk station. It went past the napkin holder.

illustration.description: Jin points excitedly at a trail of golden breadcrumbs on the bright cafeteria floor. The breadcrumbs create a winding path across the colorful tile floor, leading toward the distance. Jin's red glasses catch the light as he grins with discovery.
illustration.alt: Jin discovering a trail of breadcrumbs on the floor

pages.text: "Where does it go?" asked Elio. The friends followed the crumb trail. It led to the playground door. Zara opened the door slowly. "What if it's a monster?" whispered Maya. But Jin giggled. "Monsters don't eat sandwiches, Maya!"

illustration.description: The four friends huddle together at a bright red playground door, with Zara's hand on the handle. Maya looks slightly worried while Jin giggles. The breadcrumb trail continues under the door, and warm sunlight streams in from outside.
illustration.alt: Friends following the trail to the playground door

pages.text: Outside on the playground, they saw something funny. A small brown squirrel sat by the swings. The squirrel was holding Elio's sandwich! It was much too big for the tiny squirrel.

illustration.description: A small, fluffy brown squirrel with big bright eyes sits on green grass near colorful playground swings, holding a large peanut butter and jelly sandwich that's almost as big as the squirrel itself. The scene is whimsical and warm with bright playground equipment in the background.
illustration.alt: A squirrel holding Elio's large sandwich by the playground swings

pages.text: "That's my sandwich!" said Elio. But he wasn't angry. The squirrel looked very hungry. It had probably not eaten all day. "Wait," said Elio. "Are you hungry, little guy?"

illustration.description: Elio kneels down near the squirrel with a gentle, understanding expression on his face. The squirrel looks up at him with big innocent eyes, still clutching the sandwich. The other three friends watch curiously from behind Elio.
illustration.alt: Elio speaking gently to the hungry squirrel

pages.text: The squirrel nodded its little head. It took a tiny bite of the sandwich. Then it chittered happily. "He was just hungry," said Zara. "Like us!" The friends all laughed. Even hungry squirrels need lunch!

illustration.description: The adorable squirrel takes a small bite from the sandwich, with crumbs around its tiny mouth and cheeks puffed out. All four children are laughing joyfully, their faces bright with amusement and understanding. The scene is warm and heartwarming.
illustration.alt: The squirrel eating and the children laughing together

pages.text: "I have an idea," said Maya. She ran back inside. She came back with crackers from her lunch. Jin brought apple slices. Zara brought her extra cookies. They made a picnic for everyone!

illustration.description: The three friends return carrying various colorful lunch items - crackers, bright red apple slices, and chocolate chip cookies. They're arranging the food on the green grass in a circle, creating an impromptu picnic spread with vibrant colors.
illustration.alt: Friends bringing food to share for an outdoor picnic

pages.text: Elio broke his sandwich into pieces. He gave some to his friends. He gave some to the squirrel. Everyone sat in a circle on the grass. They shared their lunch together. The squirrel sat right next to Elio.

illustration.description: All four diverse children sit cross-legged in a circle on bright green grass, sharing colorful food pieces. The friendly brown squirrel sits contentedly next to Elio, holding a small piece of sandwich. Everyone looks happy and peaceful in the warm sunshine.
illustration.alt: Everyone sitting in a circle sharing lunch, including the squirrel

pages.text: "This is the best lunch ever!" said Elio. The squirrel chittered in agreement. Sometimes the best mysteries have the happiest endings. And sometimes the best lunches are the ones you share with friends. Even furry ones!

illustration.description: A heartwarming scene showing all four smiling children and the content squirrel finishing their shared meal. The squirrel has crumbs on its whiskers and everyone looks satisfied and happy. The playground and school building create a cheerful background in warm, golden light.
illustration.alt: Happy ending with everyone satisfied after sharing lunch together

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was missing from Elio's lunchbox?
questions.explanation: Elio's sandwich was missing from his blue lunchbox, which made him very surprised!

questions.question: Who helped Elio look for his lunch?
questions.explanation: Maya, Jin, and Zara were Elio's friends who helped him become detectives to find his missing lunch.

questions.question: What clue did Jin find on the floor?
questions.explanation: Jin found breadcrumbs that made a trail leading to the playground door.

questions.question: Who really took Elio's sandwich?
questions.explanation: A small brown squirrel had taken the sandwich because it was hungry and needed food.

questions.question: How did the story end?
questions.explanation: Everyone shared their lunch together in a circle on the grass, including the friendly squirrel.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.instructions: Choose the best word to complete each sentence from the story.

sentences.text: Elio opened his blue _____ and found it empty.
Jin found _____ on the floor that made a trail.
The hungry _____ was holding Elio's sandwich.

pages.title: Story Sequence
pages.instructions: Put these events in the right order from the story.

events.text: Everyone shared lunch together on the grass
Elio discovered his sandwich was missing
The friends followed breadcrumbs to the playground
They found the squirrel with the sandwich
